hero.title:
Quarterly Employment
Law Updates
hero.description: Employment law moves fast. Four times a year, the same practicing attorneys who teach our certificate programs deliver 90-minute sessions on the most critical developments. NLRB rulings, state law changes, Supreme Court decisions, and emerging compliance risks. Stay current without leaving your desk.
hero.alumniNote: IAML Alumni: Free access included with your certificate program.

contentSection.headline: Stay Current Without the Time Commitment
contentSection.description: Each quarterly session delivers 90 focused minutes on the employment law developments that actually matter to your organization. No fluff, no theory - just practical updates from attorneys who handle these issues daily.
benefits: Practicing attorneys, not academics - Your instructors handle employment law cases daily at firms like Taft, Thompson Hine, and Barnes & Thornburg. They teach from real courtrooms and boardrooms, not textbooks.
Curated content, not information overload - We filter the noise and focus on the developments that actually affect your workplace. No padding, just substance.
Practical strategies you can implement - Each session includes specific action items you can apply immediately, not just legal theory.
CE credits without the travel - Earn 1.5 SHRM PDCs, HRCI recertification credits, and CLE credits from your desk.

topicAreas.title: NLRB & Labor Relations
topicAreas.description: New decisions, enforcement priorities, and what they mean for your workplace policies and practices.

topicAreas.title: State Law Updates

topicAreas.title: Federal Developments
topicAreas.description: Supreme Court decisions, DOL and EEOC guidance, and regulatory changes affecting employers.

topicAreas.title: Discrimination & Harassment
topicAreas.description: New case law, enforcement trends, and practical prevention strategies across all protected classes.

topicAreas.title: Wage & Hour
topicAreas.description: FLSA developments, overtime changes, and classification issues affecting compensation compliance.

topicAreas.title: Emerging Issues
topicAreas.description: AI hiring regulations, pay transparency, remote work policies, and other developments shaping HR strategy.

faq.question: Am I eligible for free alumni access?
faq.answer: If you've attended any IAML certificate program, you're eligible for complimentary access to Quarterly Employment Law Updates for 12 months following your program. This benefit is part of your program investment and reflects our commitment to ongoing support.

faq.question: What if I miss a live session?
faq.answer: Recordings are available within 24 hours of each session. You can watch at your convenience while still earning CE credits.

faq.question: Do I earn continuing education credits?
faq.answer: Yes. Each 90-minute session provides 1.5 SHRM PDCs, 1.5 HRCI recertification credits, and 1.5 CLE credits (where applicable).

faq.question: Can I share my access with colleagues?
faq.answer: Registration is individual. However, your colleagues can register for $397/session, or if they attend an IAML certificate program, they'll receive complimentary access as alumni.

faq.question: What topics are covered in each session?
faq.answer: Topics vary by quarter based on the most significant employment law developments. Recent sessions have covered NLRB's Cemex and Stericycle decisions, state law updates from California and New York, and emerging AI hiring regulations. We focus on what you need to know now.

faq.question: How long is each session?
faq.answer: Each live session runs 90 minutes, including Q&A with our faculty. This format provides enough depth to be actionable without consuming your entire day.

faq.question: Is this the same content as your certificate programs?
faq.answer: No. Quarterly updates focus exclusively on recent developments and emerging trends. They complement the foundational expertise you gain from certificate programs by keeping you current as the law evolves.

faq.question: When are the sessions held?
faq.answer: Sessions are typically held mid-quarter (January, April, July, October) to provide timely coverage of developments from the previous quarter. Exact dates are announced 4-6 weeks in advance.

facultySection.headline: Practicing Attorneys, Not Academics
facultySection.subtitle: The same faculty who teach our certificate programs deliver your quarterly updates

finalCta.headline: Don't Let Employment Law Changes Catch You Off Guard
finalCta.body: Employment law moves fast. New NLRB decisions, state legislature activity, and agency guidance can change the rules overnight. Quarterly updates from practicing attorneys keep you current and confident.
